walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Caverns
walkthrough.text: Vast: The Crystal Caverns is a wildly asymmetric game where every player has a completely different role, different rules, and a different win condition. There are five roles: the Knight, the Goblins, the Dragon, the Cave, and the Thief. Each one plays like its own mini-game, so setup is a bit involved. Don't worry — we'll walk through it step by step. For your first game, the rulebook recommends all roles except the Thief.

walkthrough.title: Choose Roles and Seating Order
walkthrough.text: With 5 players, use all five roles. With 4, drop the Thief. With 3, check the variant cards for your combination — the recommended 3-player setup is Knight vs. Goblins vs. Dragon. With 2, you'll use special variant cards too. Seat yourselves clockwise in this order: Knight, Goblins, Dragon, Cave, Thief. Turn order always follows this sequence.

walkthrough.title: Build the Starting Cave
walkthrough.text: Place the Entrance tile in the center of the table. This is where everything begins. Set aside the 9 Crystal tiles and 6 Vault tiles (return Vaults to the box if there's no Thief). Shuffle all the remaining Cave tiles, then place four of them Dark side up adjacent to the Entrance tile — one on each cardinal side. These are the first unexplored areas of the cave.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Cave Tile Stacks
walkthrough.text: Divide the remaining shuffled tiles evenly into 3 piles. Shuffle 3 Crystal tiles and 2 Vault tiles (if using Thief) into each pile, then stack the piles on top of each other. This forms the Cave tile draw stack. Place the Cave Reference card showing 'The Cave Grows...' where everyone can see it. This card reminds all players how tiles get placed during the game.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Knight
walkthrough.text: The Knight player takes their board, 7 Hero cubes (yellow), 3 Bomb tokens, and the Knight piece. Set Health to 7 and Grit to 0. Place the Knight piece on the Entrance tile. Place 2 Hero cubes near your board (these are your starting cubes), and place the remaining 5 on the white spaces of the Grit track at positions 5, 11, 18, 26, and 35. Shuffle the Sidequest deck and draw 3 cards into your hand.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Goblins
walkthrough.text: The Goblins player takes their board, 12 Goblin discs (green), 4 Strength discs (red), 3 Goblin Tribe pieces, and the Rage token. Place all 3 Tribe pieces on your player board — they start hidden. Place 1 Strength disc on the Fangs Tribe space. Shuffle the War, Monster, and Secrets decks separately and place them near your board. Set your Rage to 1.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Dragon
walkthrough.text: The Dragon player takes their board, 14 Sloth cubes (dark red), 18 Power cards, the Dragon die, the Shriek token, the Flamewall token, 3 Dragon Gem tokens, and both Dragon pieces. Place the Sloth cubes in the Sloth boxes on your board across the Greed, Hunger, and Pride tracks. Set Health to 5. Shuffle the Power cards and draw 3. Place the Awakened Dragon piece nearby — you start underground. On your first turn, place the Sleeping Dragon on the Knight's tile.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Cave
walkthrough.text: The Cave player takes their board, all the Omen tokens (put them in the draw bag), Crystal tokens, Event tokens, Rockslide tokens, and the Event and Treasure card decks. Place 10 Treasure tokens on your board (12 if there's a Thief). Shuffle both card decks. Draw 3 Cave tiles from the stack into your hand. You'll be the one shaping the map and managing the cave's many dangers.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Thief (If Playing)
walkthrough.text: The Thief player takes their board, 3 Stat tokens (light-gray side up), the Action die, 5 Action cubes, 1 Thief piece, the Loot Drop token, and the 6 Vault tokens (place them near your board). Set the Loot Drop Level to 3. Before your first turn, place the Thief piece on the Entrance tile. Your Stat tokens start on their light-gray sides — you'll flip them as you upgrade.

walkthrough.title: Understand the Win Conditions
walkthrough.text: This is the heart of the game — every role wins differently. The Knight must kill the Dragon (reduce its Health to 0). The Goblins must kill the Knight (reduce her Health to 0). The Dragon must remove 11 Sloth cubes to awaken, surface on a Crystal tile, then escape via the Entrance. The Cave must place all tiles and then collapse 5 Crystal tiles. The Thief must acquire and stash 6 Treasure or Dragon Gem tokens. Everyone is playing a different game on the same board.

walkthrough.title: Important: The Cave Always Matters
walkthrough.text: Even if nobody is playing the Cave role, the Cave can still collapse and cause everyone to lose. When there's no Cave player, each player places 1 tile at the end of their own turn, and during the Collapse each player removes 3 tiles at the end of their turn. The Cave Reference card tracks this. Always keep an eye on how many tiles are left — the Collapse is a ticking clock for everyone.

walkthrough.title: Review the Player Boards
walkthrough.text: Each player board is packed with information. Take a moment to read yours. The Knight's board shows her statistics (Movement, Perception, Strength), equipment, and Grit track. The Goblins' board shows Tribe stats, Rage, and the War card slot. The Dragon's board shows Sloth tracks, powers, and Wakefulness. The Cave's board shows omens and their costs. The Thief's board shows Stat tokens, actions, and upgrade spaces. Your board is your reference for everything.

walkthrough.title: Final Check Before Playing
walkthrough.text: Double-check: Entrance tile in the center with 4 Dark tiles around it. Knight on the Entrance. Goblin Tribes hidden on their board. Dragon piece set aside (placed on Knight's tile at start of Dragon's first turn). Cave player has 3 tiles in hand and all tokens ready. Thief on the Entrance tile if playing. Cave Reference card visible. Each player has their decks shuffled and starting hands drawn. The Knight goes first, then clockwise: Goblins, Dragon, Cave, Thief.

walkthrough.title: The Big Picture
walkthrough.text: Vast is not like most games. There is no shared ruleset that everyone follows. Each of the five roles has its own turn structure, its own actions, and its own win condition. The Knight explores, the Goblins ambush, the Dragon slumbers, the Cave shapes the map, and the Thief sneaks around stealing everything. The game ends instantly when any player achieves their win condition — or when the Cave collapses and buries everyone.

walkthrough.title: The Knight's Turn
walkthrough.text: The Knight's turn has two phases: Pick Up Hero Cubes, then Move and Act. Hero cubes are the Knight's currency — you allocate them to your three statistics (Movement, Perception, Strength) and your equipment (Bomb, Bow, Ancient Map, Shield). All stats start at 1 each turn. Movement determines how many tiles you can enter. Perception determines how many Encounters you can have. Strength determines your combat power. You can allocate cubes any time during your turn, and they stay placed until your next turn.

walkthrough.title: The Knight's Encounters
walkthrough.text: When the Knight spends an Encounter, she can: reveal and resolve a Dark tile (required if entering one), attack enemies on her tile, smash Crystal tokens, or collect Treasures and Dragon Gems. The order matters — first reveal the tile, then resolve attacks, then collect items. Entering a Dark tile flips it to its Lit side and gains you 1 Grit. Different tile types trigger different effects: Event tiles mean the Cave plays an Event card on you, Ambush tiles let the Goblins attack, Treasure Rooms place a Treasure token, and Crystal tiles place a Crystal token.

walkthrough.title: The Knight's Grit and Equipment
walkthrough.text: Grit is the Knight's experience tracker. You gain Grit by exploring tiles (+1), smashing Crystals (+2), collecting Dragon Gems (+5 or -2 depending on a die roll), completing Sidequests (+3 to +6), and more. As Grit rises, you unlock new Hero cubes at positions 5, 11, 18, 26, and 35 on the track. More cubes means higher stats and more equipment. Your equipment includes: the Bomb (attack the Dragon underground or blow up walls), the Bow (shoot a visible Goblin Tribe), the Ancient Map (move through walls), and the Shield (prevent forced movement, reduce Grit loss to 1).

walkthrough.title: The Goblins' Turn
walkthrough.text: The Goblins manage three distinct Tribes — Fangs, Bones, and Eye — each with different abilities. Your turn has five phases: Choose War Card, Populate Tribes, Assign Monsters, Draw Secrets, and Activate Tribes. First, if your Rage is 0, it becomes 1. Draw War cards equal to your Rage (1-3), pick one to resolve, and discard the rest. The chosen War card tells you how many Goblin discs to add to each Tribe, how many Monsters to draw, and how many Secrets cards to draw.

walkthrough.title: The Goblins' Tribes and Rage
walkthrough.text: A Tribe's Population equals its Goblin discs (max 4). Its Strength equals Population plus any Strength discs. A Tribe can Attack (enter a tile with the Knight to deal 1 Health damage, then scatter), Plunder (take a Treasure token for +1 Rage), Explore (place a new Dark tile), Reveal (enter the map from hiding), or Hide (return to your board). The Fangs Tribe has a permanent +1 Strength disc and can increase Rage. The Bones Tribe can hold 2 Monsters and summon more. The Eye Tribe draws Secrets cards. Rage is crucial — it determines how many War cards you draw. It goes up when you're attacked and down when you attack.

walkthrough.title: The Dragon's Turn
walkthrough.text: The Dragon's turn has four phases: Move and Use Powers, Pick Up Treasure, Place Dragon Gem, and Replace Hand. You start underground and can move 1-2 tiles in a straight line for free. Underground, you can pass through walls and enter Dark tiles. To use powers, discard Power cards matching the required symbols. Key powers include: Claw (roll die, eat Goblins, move Knight), Flame (roll die, reveal Dark tiles), Hiss (force a Goblin Tribe to hide and lose 1 Pop), Scratch (attack Knight for 5 Grit, devastate Goblins, kill Thief), and Wing (move extra distance).

walkthrough.title: The Dragon's Awakening
walkthrough.text: The Dragon must remove 11 of 14 Sloth cubes to awaken. Sloth cubes sit on three tracks — Greed (4 cubes, removed by picking up Treasures), Hunger (4 cubes, removed by eating Goblins), and Pride (6 cubes, removed by revealing Event tiles, not moving, or placing Dragon Gems). You can move at most 1 cube per track per turn, and at most 3 cubes total per turn. Once 11 cubes reach the Wakefulness track, you awaken. Then surface by ending your turn on a Crystal tile, and finally escape by reaching the Entrance tile. On the surface, you can no longer pass through walls or enter Dark tiles.

walkthrough.title: The Cave's Turn
walkthrough.text: The Cave is the game's puppet master. Your turn has three phases: Collect Omen Tokens, Shape the Cave, and Place Treasure. You draw Omen tokens based on how many Treasure tokens plus Crystal tokens are on the map (0 tokens = 1 omen, 1 = 2, 2-3 = 3, up to 11+ = 6). Then place 1 tile from your hand, Dark side up. You must place Crystal tiles before others. Finally, place 1 Treasure token on a Dark tile without a player piece or Treasure.

walkthrough.title: The Cave's Omens
walkthrough.text: Omens are the Cave's special powers, paid for by returning Omen tokens to the draw bag. You have six omens: Giant Bats (1 omen — move a Treasure, Knight, or Goblin Tribe up to 3 spaces), Rockslide (2 omens — place a wall between tiles), Past Plunder (1/2/3 omens — place a Treasure token), Soporific Spores (3 omens — damage all players), Hatred (1/2/3 omens — place or remove a tile), and Crystal Curse (1 omen — rotate a tile, place Event token, or shuffle Event deck). You can use any omen multiple times per turn if you can pay.

walkthrough.title: The Thief's Turn
walkthrough.text: The Thief's turn has two phases: Assign Stat Tokens, then Move and Take Actions. Pick up your 3 Stat tokens and assign one each to Movement, Stealth, and Thievery. Thievery determines how many Action cubes you get. You can enter any tile including Dark tiles and tiles with other players (but not through walls unless you Climb). Your actions cost Action cubes: Loot (pick up Treasure or Dragon Gem), Climb (move through walls for 2 cubes, or impassable terrain for 3), Pickpocket (steal from another player), Pick Lock (open Vault tokens), Backstab (injure another player), and Hide Loot (reduce your Loot Drop Level).

walkthrough.title: The Thief's Stealth and Stashing
walkthrough.text: To target another player with Pickpocket or Backstab, your Stealth must be greater than their Perception (Knight/Goblins) or the Dragon's Armor. Similarly, they need Perception greater than your Stealth to target you. Each unstashed token you carry reduces your Stealth by 1, so get to the Entrance quickly to stash. Stashing upgrades your character — each stashed Treasure goes onto an upgrade space, permanently improving your stats or abilities. Stash 6 tokens total to win. If you're killed, drop all unstashed tokens and respawn at the Entrance next turn.

walkthrough.title: The Collapse
walkthrough.text: Once all Cave tiles have been placed, the Collapse begins on the next player's turn. During the Collapse, the Cave player removes 3 tiles per turn instead of placing them (or each player removes 3 if there's no Cave player). Tiles touching only 1 other tile must be removed first, then tiles touching only 2. Revealed Crystal tiles must be removed before other eligible tiles. If 5 Crystal tiles are collapsed, the Cave wins and everyone else loses. The Collapse is a ticking clock that creates urgency for every player.

walkthrough.title: The Dragon Die
walkthrough.text: Many effects require rolling the Dragon die. It's an 8-sided spatial die — the center mark represents the roller's current tile, and the surrounding 8 marks represent the 8 surrounding tiles. When you roll, apply the effect to all white spaces shown on the die face. Three of the six faces show the center space marked. This die is used for the Dragon's Claw, Flame, and Wrath powers, for Dragon Gem traps, and for the Thief's action die. It's a core randomizer that creates tension throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: General Rules for All Players
walkthrough.text: A few universal rules tie everything together. Adjacent means the four cardinal directions only — never diagonal. Distances are always counted orthogonally. When a Dark tile is revealed, orient it so at least one open edge connects to the Entrance through open edges, then fill new open edges with Dark tiles. All components are public information except cards in players' hands and unrevealed tile faces. Forced movement follows special rules for each role — the Knight can't be moved through walls, Goblins can't be moved onto other players, and so on. If a rule on a card contradicts the rulebook, follow the card.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Tutorial
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we'll walk through a scripted first round with all five roles taking turns. Even if you're playing with fewer roles, follow along — seeing how every role interacts is the key to understanding Vast. We'll call our players by their role names. Ready? The Knight goes first.

walkthrough.title: The Knight's First Turn: Pick Up Cubes
walkthrough.text: Knight, pick up your 2 starting Hero cubes. You have no cubes on the Grit track yet (you need 5 Grit to unlock the first one), so these two are all you've got. Think about what you need this turn. You want to explore — that means Movement and Perception. Place 1 cube on Movement (now Movement 2) and you'll save the other for later. Your stats are now Movement 2, Perception 1, Strength 1.

walkthrough.title: The Knight Explores
walkthrough.text: With Movement 2, you can enter 2 tiles. Step onto one of the 4 Dark tiles adjacent to the Entrance. Since it's Dark, you must spend your 1 Encounter to reveal it. Flip it over, orient it so at least one open edge connects back to the Entrance, and the Cave fills any new open edges with Dark tiles. You gain 1 Grit. If it's an Event tile, the Cave plays an Event card on you. If it's a Treasure Room, place a Treasure token. Nice — you've started exploring!

walkthrough.title: Knight Tip: Save That Second Cube
walkthrough.text: You still have 1 unplaced Hero cube. Place it on your Strength track before ending your turn. Even though you're not attacking anything yet, having Strength 2 means the Goblins can't ambush you with a 1-Population Tribe. Always end your turn with some Strength as insurance. You can assign unused cubes to any stat or equipment at any time — even at the end of your turn.

walkthrough.title: The Goblins' First Turn: War Card
walkthrough.text: Goblins, your Rage starts at 1, so you draw 1 War card — no choices yet. Look at the card: it shows how many Goblin discs to add to each Tribe (Fangs gets a number, Bones gets a number, Eye gets a number), plus how many Monsters and Secrets to draw. Add the indicated discs under each Tribe piece on your board. Remember, max 4 Population per Tribe.

walkthrough.title: The Goblins Deploy
walkthrough.text: Draw Monsters equal to the War card's number and assign them to Tribes (Fangs and Eye hold 1 each, Bones holds 2). Draw your Secrets cards. Now activate your Tribes. Since all three are hidden on your board, your main option is to Reveal them — place a Tribe piece on a Dark tile that shows that Tribe's symbol. Reveal the Fangs Tribe onto a Fangs-symbol Dark tile. It's now on the map, hidden in the darkness, waiting for the Knight.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: Revealing on the Wrong Tile
walkthrough.text: New Goblin players sometimes try to reveal a Tribe onto any Dark tile. You can only reveal a Tribe onto a Dark tile that shows THAT Tribe's specific symbol on its Dark side. If there are no matching tiles on the map, you can instead place the Tribe on an open space adjacent to any tile — this is called 'lurking.' A lurking Tribe is hidden but vulnerable if a tile gets placed under it. Always check the Dark tile symbols before revealing.

walkthrough.title: The Dragon's First Turn: Placement
walkthrough.text: Dragon, this is your very first turn. Place the Sleeping Dragon piece on the same tile as the Knight. Yes, you start right on top of her — but don't worry, you're underground and she can't reach you without a Bomb. Draw your starting 3 Power cards if you haven't already. Look at the symbols on your cards: Claw, Flame, Wing, and so on. These are the currency for your powers.

walkthrough.title: The Dragon Moves Underground
walkthrough.text: You get 1-2 tiles of free movement in a straight line. Since you're underground, you can pass through walls and enter Dark tiles — the whole cave is open to you. Move 2 tiles away from the Knight. You want to be near Treasure tokens (for your Greed track) and Goblin Tribes (for your Hunger track). Look at what Power cards you have and see if you can use any — maybe a Flame to reveal some Dark tiles, or a Hiss to bother a Goblin Tribe.

walkthrough.title: The Dragon's Sloth Cubes
walkthrough.text: Your main goal right now is removing Sloth cubes. You need 11 of 14 to awaken. Check your Sloth tracks: did you skip your free movement this turn? If so, you can move 1 Pride cube to Wakefulness. Are there any Treasure tokens on your tile? Picking one up moves a Greed cube. You can only move 1 cube per track per turn, and 3 total. Early game, try to tick at least 1-2 cubes per turn. The faster you awaken, the sooner you can escape.

walkthrough.title: The Cave's First Turn
walkthrough.text: Cave, your turn has three parts. First, count Treasure tokens plus Crystal tokens on the map. If there are none yet, you draw 1 Omen token from the bag. Next, Shape the Cave — place 1 tile from your hand, Dark side up, adjacent to any tile on the map. If you have Crystal tiles in hand, you must place those first. Finally, place 1 Treasure token on any Dark tile without a player piece or Treasure. Draw a replacement tile from the stack.

walkthrough.title: Cave Strategy: Where to Place Tiles
walkthrough.text: Think about what helps you and hurts others. Placing tiles expands the map — but you WANT the map to expand because you need all tiles placed before the Collapse begins. Place tiles to create long paths away from the Entrance (making the Dragon's escape harder) and near Goblin-symbol tiles (giving the Goblins places to reveal). You can peek at Dark tiles on the map anytime. Use this to your advantage when placing Treasures — put them where the Dragon or Thief will have to work to reach them.

walkthrough.title: The Thief's First Turn (If Playing)
walkthrough.text: Thief, assign your 3 Stat tokens. For your first turn, a balanced spread works well: put your 2 on Movement and your 1s on Stealth and Thievery (or adjust based on token values). With Thievery determining your Action cubes, you'll get cubes equal to that value. Now move off the Entrance and look for Treasure tokens or Vault tokens to grab. Remember, you can enter Dark tiles and peek at them.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: The Thief Forgets Stealth
walkthrough.text: New Thief players often load up on Movement and Thievery while neglecting Stealth. But here's the problem: every unstashed token you carry reduces your Stealth by 1. If you grab 2 Treasures and your Stealth was only 2, your Stealth drops to 0 — meaning the Knight (Perception 1) can now attack and kill you. The Thief is fragile. Always plan your escape route before you start looting, and stash tokens at the Entrance as quickly as possible.

walkthrough.title: Round 1, Turn 2: Knight Pushes Deeper
walkthrough.text: Back to the Knight. Pick up your Hero cubes again. You should have 2 (or maybe 3 if you hit 5 Grit last turn). This time, push deeper into the cave. Allocate for Movement 2 or 3 and Perception 1-2. Explore more Dark tiles for Grit. Watch out for Ambush tiles — when you reveal one, you can immediately assign more Hero cubes to Strength before the Goblins decide whether to attack with a hidden Tribe. Smart Knights always keep a reserve.

walkthrough.title: Round 1, Turn 2: Goblins Spring the Trap
walkthrough.text: Goblins, if your Rage is still at 1, draw 1 War card again. Populate your Tribes, assign Monsters, draw Secrets. Now comes the fun part — if the Knight has revealed an Ambush tile and one of your hidden Tribes has higher Strength than her Strength, you can attack! The Knight loses 1 Health, your Tribe scatters (back to your board, lose 2 Population, discard Monster), and your Rage decreases by 1. That's a big hit — but your Tribe takes a beating too.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: Goblins Attack Too Early
walkthrough.text: It's tempting to ambush the Knight with a small Tribe on the first Ambush tile. But if your Tribe only has 2 Population, the scatter costs 2 — wiping it out completely. And your Rage drops by 1, potentially to 0 (which causes Malaise, giving all Tribes -1 Strength). A better strategy is to build up your Tribes' Population first, reveal them onto strategic tiles, and attack when a Tribe has 3 or 4 Population so it survives the scatter with some strength left.

walkthrough.title: Round 1, Turn 2: Dragon Seeks Treasure
walkthrough.text: Dragon, move toward a Treasure token. When you pick one up, give it to the Cave — this satisfies your Greed condition and moves 1 Sloth cube from the Greed track to Wakefulness. If you have cards with the Hunger symbol, look for Goblin Tribes nearby — eating 2 Goblins per Hunger condition means you need Goblins on the map. Use your Flame power to reveal Dark tiles (satisfying Pride) and scout the layout. Every turn, try to check off at least one Sloth condition.

walkthrough.title: Round 1, Turn 2: Cave Expands and Manipulates
walkthrough.text: Cave, you should have more Omen tokens now as Treasures and Crystals appear on the map. Place another tile and a Treasure. Now consider your omens — Giant Bats costs just 1 omen and can move the Knight, a Goblin Tribe, or a Treasure token up to 3 spaces. Moving the Knight onto a dangerous tile or moving a Treasure away from the Dragon can be devastating. Save your omens for big moments, but don't hoard them either.

walkthrough.title: The Interactions Begin
walkthrough.text: By now, the five roles should be colliding. The Knight is exploring and building Grit, probably close to unlocking a third Hero cube. The Goblins have Tribes on the map and are eyeing ambush opportunities. The Dragon is ticking Sloth cubes and eating stray Goblins. The Cave is shaping the map and accumulating omens. And the Thief is sneaking around collecting loot. This web of interactions is what makes Vast special — every role affects every other role.

walkthrough.title: Watching the Collapse Clock
walkthrough.text: Keep an eye on the Cave tile stack. When the Cave player places the last tile from their hand, the Collapse begins on their next turn. The Cave Reference card flips to remind everyone. During the Collapse, 3 tiles are removed per Cave turn (or per player turn without a Cave player). Crystal tiles must be removed first when eligible. Once 5 Crystal tiles are removed, the Cave wins. This clock creates urgency for everyone — especially the Dragon, who needs to awaken and escape before the cave buries everyone.

walkthrough.title: Key Timing: When Roles Become Dangerous
walkthrough.text: Each role has a power spike. The Knight becomes dangerous once she has 4-5 Hero cubes (around 18-26 Grit) — now she has high Strength and can equip the Bomb. The Goblins peak at Rage 3, drawing 3 War cards for maximum choice and Population. The Dragon is most threatening right before surfacing — once awake, the Knight can attack freely. The Cave grows stronger as the Collapse nears. The Thief gets scarier with each upgrade, especially after flipping Stat tokens to higher values.

walkthrough.title: Negotiation and Table Talk
walkthrough.text: Vast is not a cooperative game, but temporary alliances happen naturally. The Goblins and Cave both want the Knight dead (the Goblins to win, the Cave to remove a strong explorer). The Knight and Goblins both want the Dragon to stay asleep. The Thief wants everyone distracted. Talk at the table — warn someone that the Dragon is about to awaken, or remind the Cave player that collapsing too fast helps nobody. Just remember, there's only one winner.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You've seen all five roles take their first turns, watched key interactions unfold, and learned three critical mistakes to avoid. Remember: Knights should always keep Strength in reserve, Goblins shouldn't throw away Tribes on weak ambushes, and the Thief needs to manage Stealth carefully. Vast is a game that rewards experience — each play teaches you something new about how the roles interact.

walkthrough.title: Before You Descend
walkthrough.text:
Before you start a full game, here are three things that can help.

First — check out General Tips for role-specific strategy basics and more common mistakes.

Second — reference the Appendix for a quick guide to every power, omen, tile type, and key term in the game.

And most importantly — ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Good luck in the caverns!

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes Across All Roles
walkthrough.text: Before diving into strategy, here are the universal traps. First, ignoring the Collapse clock — once all tiles are placed, the Cave starts ripping the map apart. If you're not close to winning, you'll get buried. Second, forgetting that the Dragon die affects surrounding tiles, not just the center — always consider splash damage. Third, not reading other players' boards — you need to know the Knight's Strength, the Dragon's Wakefulness, and the Goblins' Rage to play well. Fourth, tunnel-visioning on your own win condition while ignoring the player about to win. Keep your head on a swivel.

walkthrough.title: Knight Tips
walkthrough.text: Your early game is all about Grit. Every Dark tile you reveal is +1 Grit, every Crystal you smash is +2. Those Hero cube unlocks at 5, 11, 18, 26, and 35 are game-changing. Prioritize exploration over combat early on. Keep your Shield equipped when you can — losing only 1 Grit from effects instead of 5 is massive. Complete Sidequests for big Grit bonuses (3-6 each). Once you have 4+ cubes, you can start seriously threatening the Dragon. Remember: you can't attack the Dragon underground without the Bomb unless the Collapse has begun.

walkthrough.title: Goblins Tips
walkthrough.text: Rage management is everything. At Rage 3, you draw 3 War cards and pick the best — this is enormously powerful. But attacking reduces Rage by 1, so you need to balance offense and Rage building. Use the Fangs Tribe's special action (+1 Rage) to stay topped up. The Plunder action also raises Rage. Don't reveal all three Tribes at once — keep at least one hidden for ambushes. The Bones Tribe with 2 Monsters can be devastating. Eye Tribe's Secrets cards include powerful one-shots like Cave-In and Hex. Save them for critical moments.

walkthrough.title: Dragon Tips
walkthrough.text: Your early game is a puzzle: how to remove Sloth cubes as efficiently as possible. Greed requires Treasure tokens — move toward them. Hunger requires eating Goblins — use Claw and Hiss. Pride is the most flexible: revealing Event tiles, not moving for a turn, or placing Dragon Gems. Don't forget you can skip your free movement to remove 1 Pride cube — sometimes not moving is the strongest play. Once awake, plan your surface route carefully. You need to end a turn on a Crystal tile, then reach the Entrance — and on the surface, you can't pass through walls.

walkthrough.title: Cave Tips
walkthrough.text: You control the map, the Events, and the Treasures. Place Crystal tiles strategically — they're both your win condition and the Dragon's surfacing points. Spread them out to make the Collapse cover more ground. During the Collapse, Crystal tiles must be removed first when eligible, so positioning matters. Use Omens wisely: Giant Bats for disruption, Rockslides to wall off escape routes, and Soporific Spores to weaken everyone at once. Remember, you can examine any Dark tile on the map — use that knowledge.

walkthrough.title: Thief Tips
walkthrough.text: Speed and stealth are your weapons. Get in, grab loot, stash at the Entrance, repeat. Each stashed Treasure gives you a permanent upgrade — prioritize upgrades that suit your strategy. Climbing Gear (reduces Climb cost) and Sticky Fingers (reroll Pickpocket) are strong early upgrades. The Flip Stat Tokens upgrades are game-changing — going from 2-value tokens to 3-value tokens means more Movement, more Stealth, and more Action cubes every turn. Always plan your stash route before committing to a heist.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Table
walkthrough.text: Vast rewards paying attention to everyone, not just your own role. If the Dragon is at 9 Wakefulness, everyone should be worried — the Dragon is about to surf and sprint for the exit. If the Goblins have Rage 3 and three Tribes on the map, the Knight needs to be careful. If the Cave is running out of tiles, the Collapse is imminent. If the Thief has 4 stashed tokens, someone needs to deal with them. The best Vast players know exactly how close each player is to winning.

walkthrough.title: Tempo and Timing
walkthrough.text: Every role has an ideal pace. The Knight wants a medium-length game — enough time to build Grit but not so long that the Cave collapses. The Goblins want the game to go quickly if the Knight is weak, or slowly if they need to build Rage. The Dragon wants time to awaken but needs to escape before the Collapse. The Cave wants the game to last exactly long enough to place all tiles and collapse 5 Crystals. The Thief needs 6 stash runs — count how many turns that takes and manage accordingly. Whoever best controls the game's tempo usually wins.

walkthrough.title: The Asymmetric Mindset
walkthrough.text: The hardest part of Vast isn't any single rule — it's that you need to understand all five roles to play any one of them well. The Knight needs to know what the Goblins' Secrets cards can do. The Goblins need to track the Dragon's Wakefulness. The Dragon needs to know when the Cave's Collapse will hit. The Cave needs to manipulate all four other players simultaneously. Take time after your first game to read the chapters for every role, even the ones you didn't play. Your second game will be dramatically better than your first.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Knight: The Grit Race
walkthrough.text: Expert Knights treat Grit like a speedometer. Your first priority is reaching Grit 5 for the third Hero cube, then 11 for the fourth. From there, the game opens up. The fastest Grit path is: reveal tiles (+1 each), smash Crystals (+2 each), decline Treasure cards (+5 each), and complete Sidequests (+3-6). Declining Treasures is counterintuitive — you lose the card — but +5 Grit is often worth more than the equipment. At 18+ Grit with 5 cubes, you can go Movement 3, Perception 2, Strength 2, plus Bomb and Shield. That's lethal.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Knight: Timing the Dragon Kill
walkthrough.text: Killing the Dragon requires Strength greater than its Armor (equal Strength forces a Dragon die roll). The Dragon starts at 1 Armor but gains more as it awakens — up to 4 Armor at full Wakefulness. You need the Bomb to attack underground unless the Collapse has begun. Once the Dragon surfaces, you don't need the Bomb and can attack multiple times per turn. The optimal play is to let the Dragon surface (it has to pass through tiles where you are), then ambush it with high Strength over multiple Encounters. The Mighty Axe Treasure lets you deal 2 damage in one hit.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Goblins: Rage Engineering
walkthrough.text: At Rage 3, you draw 3 War cards and pick the best one. This is so powerful that your entire strategy should revolve around reaching and maintaining Rage 3. Ways to increase Rage: get attacked or shot (+1), use Plunder (+1), use Fangs special action (+1), kill the Thief (+1), get affected by Dragon's Hiss or Cave's Soporific Spores (+1). Ways it decreases: attack another player (-1), smash a Crystal (-1), spend to prevent overpopulation (-1). The key insight: being attacked by the Knight or Dragon is actually good for your economy. Let it happen.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Goblins: Monster Combinations
walkthrough.text: Monsters assigned to Tribes can swing combat dramatically. The Flame Giant, Ogre, and Troll all give +1 Strength (shown by a Strength disc), which means a 4-Population Tribe with a Monster can have Strength 5 — enough to overwhelm almost anything. The Bones Tribe can hold 2 Monsters, making it your heavy hitter. But remember: when a Tribe scatters, it loses its Monster. The Goblin Bats Monster lets a Tribe attack from an adjacent tile. The Blob Monster has a devastating ambush effect. Choose Monsters that match your playstyle and current board state.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Dragon: Efficient Awakening
walkthrough.text: The fastest awakening path removes 3 Sloth cubes per turn across all three tracks. The Pride track is the most flexible — skipping movement costs no cards but wastes your turn's mobility, while placing Dragon Gems when one is already on the map removes a cube for free. The trick is to stack conditions: move to a tile with both Treasure and Goblins, use Claw to eat Goblins (Hunger), pick up Treasure (Greed), and place a Gem (Pride) — that's 3 cubes in one turn. Expert Dragons awaken in 5-6 turns.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Dragon: The Surface Sprint
walkthrough.text: Once awake, you need to end your turn on a Crystal tile (to surface) and then reach the Entrance. Plan this route BEFORE awakening. On the surface, you can't move through walls or enter Dark tiles. The Wing power lets you move extra distance even on the surface. The Flame Wall power blocks a tile — use it to delay the Knight from reaching you. Remember: on the surface, entering a tile with the Knight or Goblins triggers an attack as if they attacked you. If the Knight's Strength is high, avoid her path entirely.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Cave: Controlling the Collapse
walkthrough.text: The Cave's endgame is pure spatial puzzle. You need 5 Crystal tiles removed during the Collapse. Crystal tiles must be removed first when eligible — so position them where they'll become eligible early (touching only 1-2 tiles). During the pre-Collapse, place Crystal tiles on the edges of the map. Use Rockslide tokens to create walls that isolate sections, making tiles eligible for removal sooner. The Hatred omen lets you place or remove tiles outside the normal Collapse rules — devastating when timed right. Expert Cave players can often win within 3-4 turns of the Collapse beginning.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Cave: Omen Economy
walkthrough.text: Your Omen income scales with tokens on the map. More Treasure and Crystal tokens = more Omens per turn (up to 6 at 11+ tokens). The Cave should actively manage this economy — use Past Plunder to place extra Treasures and inflate your income. Soporific Spores (3 omens) hits every player: Knight loses 5 Grit, Goblins reduce a Tribe to 1 Population, Dragon moves a Sloth cube backwards, Thief loses an upgrade. It's expensive but can reset the entire board in your favor. Time it when multiple players are close to winning.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Thief: The Stash Optimization
walkthrough.text: You need 6 stashed tokens, which means at least 6 round trips to the Entrance. The upgrade path matters enormously. Early upgrades should focus on Climbing Gear (reduces Climb from 2 cubes to 1) and Sticky Fingers (Pickpocket reroll). Mid-game, the Flip Stat Tokens upgrades are transformative — jumping from 2-value to 3-value tokens means 50% more Movement, Stealth, and Thievery every turn. The ALL 4 upgrade makes all tokens worth 4, giving you Movement 4, Stealth 4, and Thievery 4 (plus any stat boosts). At that point, you're nearly unstoppable.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Thief: Targeting the Right Player
walkthrough.text: Pickpocket and Backstab affect each role differently. Pickpocketing the Knight returns a Treasure card to its deck. Pickpocketing the Goblins steals a Secrets card. Pickpocketing the Dragon moves a Sloth cube from Wakefulness back to Greed — actively undoing the Dragon's progress. Backstab injuries scale: Light (1 cube), Moderate (2 cubes), Heavy (3 cubes), dealing Grit loss to the Knight, Population loss to Goblins, or card discard to the Dragon. Target whoever is closest to winning. The Hand Crossbow upgrade lets you Backstab from 3 spaces away — snipe from safety.

entries.definition: The heroic adventurer exploring the cave. Starts on the Entrance tile with 7 Health and 0 Grit. Uses Hero cubes to allocate stats and equipment each turn. Wins by killing the Dragon (or smashing 5 Crystals and escaping if no Dragon).

The horde of three Tribes lurking in the darkness. Manages Fangs, Bones, and Eye Tribes using War cards, Monsters, and Secrets. Wins by killing the Knight (or killing the Dragon or smashing 5 Crystals in variants).

The ancient slumbering beast beneath the cave. Starts underground with 14 Sloth cubes that must be removed to awaken. Uses Power cards for abilities. Wins by awakening (11 cubes), surfacing on a Crystal tile, and escaping via the Entrance.

The living cave itself. Shapes the map by placing tiles, controls Events and Treasures, and uses Omens for powerful effects. Wins by placing all tiles and then collapsing 5 Crystal tiles during the Collapse.

The cursed rogue seeking treasure. Uses Stat tokens for Movement, Stealth, and Thievery each turn. Can Loot, Climb, Pickpocket, Pick Lock, Backstab, and Hide Loot. Wins by stashing 6 Treasure or Dragon Gem tokens at the Entrance.

Yellow cubes used by the Knight. Allocated to statistics (Movement, Perception, Strength) and equipment (Bomb, Bow, Ancient Map, Shield) each turn. Start with 2; unlock more at Grit milestones 5, 11, 18, 26, and 35.

The Knight's experience tracker. Gained from revealing tiles (+1), smashing Crystals (+2), declining Treasures (+5), collecting Dragon Gems (+5 or -2), completing Sidequests (+3 to +6), and killing the Thief (+0 to +3). Unlocks Hero cubes at key thresholds.
Knight statistic that equals the number of adjacent tiles the Knight can move per turn. Starts at 1, increased by placing Hero cubes on the Movement track.
Knight statistic that equals the number of Encounters the Knight can have per turn, and determines interactions with the Thief. Starts at 1 each turn.
Knight statistic that determines combat power. Must exceed a Goblin Tribe's Strength to enter its tile, and must meet or exceed the Dragon's Armor to attack. Strength 3+ required to smash Crystals.

A unit of the Knight's Perception spent to interact with tiles and other players. Actions per Encounter include: revealing Dark tiles, attacking enemies, smashing Crystals, and collecting Treasures/Gems. Order: Reveal -> Attack -> Collect.

Knight equipment. Allows attacking the Dragon while underground (before Collapse begins). Can also place a Bomb token to permanently destroy a wall. The Bomb is not needed to attack the Dragon after it surfaces or after Collapse begins.

Knight equipment. Shoot a visible Goblin Tribe at unlimited range, reducing its Population by your Strength minus 1. Does not cause the Tribe to scatter. Can be used before, during, or after movement. Does not cost an Encounter.
Knight equipment. Each placed Hero cube allows the Knight to move through one wall. If both edges of two touching tiles have walls, they count as a single wall. Does not affect Movement cost.
Knight equipment. Prevents other players' effects that would move you. Each time you lose Grit, reduces the loss to 1. Must be activated with a Hero cube before the effect occurs.

Knight cards that award Grit when their conditions are met. Reveal during your turn if you satisfy the requirements, remove from game, gain Grit equal to the card's value (3-6). Draw a new Sidequest if any remain in the deck.

Goblin Tribe known for fierce warriors. Has a permanent +1 Strength disc that cannot be lost. Special action: increase Rage by 1. The Fangs Tribe's Strength equals its Population plus the Strength disc.

Goblin Tribe that controls Monsters. Can hold up to 2 Monster cards (other Tribes hold 1). Special action: draw and assign 1 Monster card. The max Monsters assigned across all Tribes equals the Bones Tribe's Population.
Goblin Tribe known for intelligence and secrets. Special action: draw 1 Secrets card. Has special interactions with certain Secrets cards. Perception equals Population plus 1.

Goblin cards drawn at the start of each turn. Draw cards equal to Rage (1-3), choose 1, discard the rest. Determines Population growth per Tribe, number of Monsters to draw, and number of Secrets to draw. Deck is reshuffled at end of turn.

Cards assigned to Goblin Tribes for combat bonuses. Some grant +1 Strength (Flame Giant, Ogre, Troll). Others have special effects like attacking from adjacent tiles (Goblin Bats). Discarded when a Tribe scatters. Max Monsters across all Tribes equals Bones Population.

Powerful Goblin one-shot cards playable any time during the Goblins' turn. Include Cave-In (remove a tile), Hex (damage the Knight for 14 Grit), Hiding Spots, Goblin Ruby, and more. Hand limit of 5 cards.

The Goblins' collective anger meter (0-3). Determines how many War cards you draw per turn. Increases when attacked, using Plunder, Fangs special action, killing Thief. Decreases when attacking, smashing Crystals, or spending to prevent overpopulation. At 0 Rage, Malaise causes all Tribes -1 Strength.
When Goblin Rage drops to 0, all Tribes suffer -1 Strength. Malaise ends immediately when Rage increases to 1 or higher. If Rage is 0 at the start of your turn, it automatically becomes 1.

When a Goblin Tribe attacks, gets attacked, or reaches 0 Population, it scatters. The Tribe piece returns to the player board, Population decreases by 2, and its Monster card is discarded. Scattered Tribes are hidden.

If any Tribe's Population would exceed 4, overpopulation triggers: reduce all over-4 Tribes to 4 Population, then scatter one revealed Tribe (or a hidden Tribe if none are revealed). Can be prevented by spending 1 Rage per affected Tribe.

Goblin action: a Tribe takes a Treasure token or Dragon Gem on its tile. Treasure: increase Rage by 1, return token to Cave. Dragon Gem: roll die (center = scatter, otherwise +1 Rage), return Gem to Dragon. Can plunder Dragon directly if no Gems on map and Tribe Strength > Dragon Armor.
Goblin action for Tribes on Dark tiles. Select an adjacent open space, and the Cave places a new Dark tile there. The exploring Tribe then moves onto that tile. Allows the Goblins to expand the map.

Dark red cubes on the Dragon's board representing its slumber. 14 total across Greed (4), Hunger (4), and Pride (6) tracks. Remove by fulfilling conditions. Move to Wakefulness track. Need 11 on Wakefulness to awaken.
Dragon track where Sloth cubes accumulate as conditions are met. At 11+ cubes, the Dragon awakens. Also determines Armor and Spirit statistics. Higher Wakefulness = stronger Dragon.

Dragon defense stat determined by Wakefulness level. Ranges from 1 (early) to 4 (max Wakefulness). The Knight's Strength must be equal to or greater than Armor to attack; Goblin Tribes need Strength greater than Armor.
Dragon stat that equals the number of Power cards drawn at end of each turn. Ranges from 3 to 7 based on Wakefulness. Higher Spirit means more options each turn.

Dragon cards used to fuel abilities. Each shows a symbol (Claw, Flame, Wing, etc.). Discard cards matching the required symbols to use a power. Hand is replaced each turn equal to Spirit stat. Dragon Gems on the map also provide spendable symbols.

Dragon power. Roll the Dragon die. All affected Goblin Tribes scatter and you eat Goblins equal to their scattered Population. You may move the Knight by 1 space. The Thief is killed. Costs 1 Claw symbol.
Dragon power. Roll the die. Reveal all affected Dark tiles. Costs 1 Flame symbol. Useful for scouting the map and satisfying Pride conditions when revealing Event tiles.
Dragon power. Move up to 2 tiles in a straight line, or move 1 tile through a wall. Costs 1 Wing symbol. Extends your mobility beyond the free 1-2 tile movement.
Dragon power. Force a Goblin Tribe anywhere on the map to become hidden and lose 1 Population. You eat 1 Goblin. Costs 1 Claw + 1 symbol (any). Does not require being on the same tile.
Dragon power. Move a Sloth cube from one Sloth track to another Sloth track that has an empty space. Costs 1 Claw + 1 symbol. Useful for optimizing which conditions you fulfill.

Dragon power. Attack the Knight (lose 5 Grit), all Goblin Tribes on your tile (reduce to 0 Pop, eat them all), or the Thief (killed) on your tile. Costs 2 Claw + 1 symbol. Very powerful but expensive.
Dragon power. Place the Shriek token to hide the cost of this ability. When rolling the Dragon die, spend the placed Shriek token to reroll. Costs 1 symbol + 1 symbol (any two). Mind game element.
Dragon power. Move the Knight, from a space adjacent to you, up to 3 spaces. Costs 1 Claw + 1 symbol. Useful for pushing the Knight away from Crystal tiles or toward Goblin ambushes.
Dragon power. Reveal all surrounding Dark tiles (all 8 spaces). Costs 1 Flame + 1 symbol. Massive map reveal that can satisfy multiple Pride conditions at once.
Dragon power. Reveal a Dark tile anywhere on the map. Costs 1 Flame + 1 Wing. Useful for scouting distant areas.
Dragon power. Move a Dragon Gem or Treasure token on an adjacent space by 5 spaces in any directions. Costs 1 Wing + 1 symbol. Repositions tokens to better locations.

Dragon power. Place the Flame Wall token on a visible adjacent tile. If entered: Knight loses 5 Grit, Goblin Tribe loses 1 Pop, Thief is killed. Dragon is not damaged. Returned to supply at start of your next turn. Costs 2 symbols + 1 Flame.

Dragon power. Smash a Crystal token on your tile, removing it from the game. Costs 3 symbols + question mark. Removes a Crystal the Knight needs and a tile the Dragon could surface on — use carefully.

Dragon power. Roll the Dragon die. All affected tiles: collapse the tile, scatter all Goblin Tribes, move all other player pieces to an adjacent space. If map splits, reconnect. Costs 4 symbols + question mark. The Dragon's ultimate power.

Tokens placed by the Dragon on Cave tiles. Each provides a spendable Power symbol (once per turn) for the Dragon's abilities. When picked up by other players, triggers a Dragon die roll with different effects per role. Returns to Dragon's supply after being collected.

An 8-sided spatial die. The center mark = roller's tile; 8 surrounding marks = adjacent tiles. Apply effects to all white spaces on the rolled face. 3 of 6 faces show the center marked. Used for Dragon powers, Dragon Gem traps, and various game effects.
Dragon track recording total Goblins eaten via Claw, Scratch, and Hiss powers. For each 2 eaten Goblins, 1 Hunger Sloth cube can be moved to Wakefulness. Track goes from 0 to 7+.
Tokens drawn by the Cave from a bag each turn. Different symbols correspond to different omen costs. Spent by returning to the bag to activate omens. Income based on Treasure + Crystal tokens on map.
Cave omen costing 1 Omen. Target a Treasure token, the Knight, or a Goblin Tribe, and move it up to 3 spaces. Versatile disruption tool.

Cave omen costing 2 Omens. Place a Rockslide token on the edge between two adjacent tiles without walls. The token acts as a wall. If all 3 tokens are placed, you may move one instead. Removed if either tile is rotated or removed.

Cave omen costing 3 Omens. Targets a player: Knight loses 5 Grit, Goblins reduce a Tribe to 1 Pop, Dragon moves a Sloth cube back from Wakefulness, Thief loses an upgrade (keeps the token). Target chooses the affected token/Tribe.
Cave omen with scaling cost (1/2/3 for first/second/third use per turn). Place a tile during normal play, or remove a tile during the Collapse. Powerful tempo accelerator.
Cave omen with scaling cost (1/2/3). Place a Treasure token following normal placement rules. Increases tokens on map, which in turn increases future Omen income.
Cave omen costing 1 Omen. Rotate a tile to any orthogonal orientation, place an Event token on an empty Event tile, or move the top 3 Event deck cards to the bottom. Flexible disruption.

Begins when all Cave tiles have been placed. The Cave removes 3 tiles per turn (or each player removes 3 without a Cave player). Remove tiles touching only 1 tile first, then only 2. Revealed Crystal tiles must be removed first. 5 Crystal tiles collapsed = Cave wins.

Three tokens assigned each turn to Movement, Stealth, and Thievery. Start with values 1, 1, 2 (gray side). Can be flipped to 2, 3, 3 (black side) via upgrades. The ALL 4 upgrade makes all tokens worth 4.
Thief stat that determines ability to avoid detection and target other players. Must exceed other players' Perception to Pickpocket/Backstab them. Each unstashed token reduces Stealth by 1.
Thief stat that equals the number of Action cubes received each turn. Higher Thievery = more actions per turn. Increased by Stat token assignment and permanent +1 Thievery upgrade.
Cubes spent by the Thief to perform actions. Number per turn equals Thievery stat. Each action costs 1-3 cubes; spending more improves effectiveness or guarantees success.
Thief action: collect a Treasure or Dragon Gem on your space. 1 cube to take; if Dragon Gem, roll Dragon die (center = killed). 2 cubes for a Gem: take without rolling (disarm the trap).
Thief action: 2 cubes to move through a wall; 3 cubes to move through impassable terrain (without stopping on it). Upgrade: Climbing Gear reduces cost by 1 cube.

Thief action: attack to steal from a player on your space. 1 cube: roll, succeed on 4+. 2 cubes: succeed on 2+. 3 cubes: auto-success. On success: gain Treasure token from Cave + harm target (Knight loses Treasure card, Goblins lose Secrets card, Dragon moves Sloth cube backwards).
Thief action: open a Vault token on your space. 1 cube: roll, succeed on 4+. 2 cubes: succeed on 2+. 3 cubes: auto. On success: remove Vault, gain Treasure from Cave supply. Once per Vault per turn.

Thief action: injure a player on your space. 1 cube: Light injury. 2 cubes: Moderate. 3 cubes: Heavy. Knight loses 1/3/5 Grit. Goblins lose 1/2/3 Population. Dragon discards 1/2/3 Power cards. Upgrade: Hand Crossbow allows targeting visible players up to 3 spaces away.
Thief action: spend X Action cubes to reduce your Loot Drop Level by X. Lower Loot Drop means less reward for other players if they kill you. Level goes from 3 (max) to 0 (no bonus).

Thief meter (0-3) that determines the bonus other players get for killing you. Level 3: Knight gets +3 Grit, Goblins get 3 Secrets/+3 Rage, Dragon draws 3 Power cards. Level 0: no bonus. Resets to 3 when you stash a Treasure.

When the Thief enters the Entrance tile, all carried Treasure and Dragon Gem tokens are stashed. Each stashed Treasure goes on an upgrade space, permanently improving the Thief. Stashed tokens cannot be lost. Stash 6 to win.
Thief upgrade. The first time each turn you are attacked, roll the Action die. On 4+, the attack fails and you may immediately move 1 space. Provides survivability against the Knight and Goblins.
The central tile where the game begins. The Knight starts here. The Dragon must reach it to escape and win. The Thief stashes loot here. Can never be removed during the Collapse.

Unrevealed Cave tiles showing a Goblin Tribe symbol on the back. Flipped to Lit side when revealed by the Knight entering, Dragon using powers, or other effects. When revealed, orient to connect to Entrance, then fill open edges.
A revealed Cave tile showing its terrain type and features. Goblin Tribes lose 1 Population for every 2 Lit tiles they exit during movement. The surface Dragon cannot enter Dark tiles, only Lit.
A Cave tile type. When revealed by the Knight, the Cave draws 3 Event cards, chooses 1 for the Knight to resolve, and puts the other 2 on the bottom of the Event deck. 15 Event tiles in the game.

A Cave tile type. When the Knight reveals one, she may immediately assign more Hero cubes to Strength. Then the Goblins may attack with a hidden Tribe if its Strength exceeds the Knight's. 15 Ambush tiles in the game.

A Cave tile type. When revealed, a Crystal token is placed on it. The Knight can smash Crystals (Strength 3+) for +2 Grit. The Dragon surfaces by ending a turn on a Crystal tile. 9 Crystal tiles total. Collapsing 5 wins the game for the Cave.
A Cave tile type. When revealed, a Treasure token is placed on it. The Knight can collect it (as an Encounter) to receive a Treasure card from the Cave. 6 Treasure Room tiles in the game.
A Cave tile type used only in games with the Thief. When revealed, a Vault token is placed on it. The Thief can Pick Lock to open it and gain a Treasure token. 6 Vault tiles in the game.

Tokens placed on the map by the Cave. Different roles interact with them differently: Knight gets Treasure cards, Goblins get +1 Rage, Dragon removes Greed cubes, Thief carries and stashes them. Unstashed tokens on the map count for Cave's Omen income.

Tokens placed on Crystal tiles when revealed. The Knight smashes them for +2 Grit (Strength 3+ required). Goblins can smash with 2 Tribes of Strength 3+ each. Count toward Cave's Omen income and Collapse victory condition.

Placed on tiles by the Cave via Crystal Curse omen or when the Dragon reveals an Event tile. If the Knight enters or starts on a tile with an Event token, she must resolve it as an Encounter before doing anything else.
Placed on Vault tiles when revealed. Only the Thief can interact with them via the Pick Lock action. Success removes the token and grants a Treasure from the Cave's supply.

Placed by the Cave's Rockslide omen on the border between two tiles. Acts as a wall. Maximum 3 on the map. If all 3 are placed, the Cave may move one. Removed if either adjacent tile is rotated or removed.

Knight tokens (3 total). Can be placed on walls to permanently destroy them. Placed by activating the Bomb equipment with a Hero cube. If the tile with a Bomb is rotated or affected by Rockslide, the Bomb is removed from the game.
Dragon token used with the Shriek power. Placed to hide the power's cost. Can be spent when rolling the Dragon die to reroll it. Returns after use.

Dragon token placed on a visible adjacent tile via the Flame Wall power. Damages anyone who enters (Knight: -5 Grit, Goblins: -1 Pop, Thief: killed). Dragon is immune. Returned to supply at start of Dragon's next turn.

Cards drawn by the Cave when the Knight collects a Treasure token. Cave draws 2, gives 1 to the Knight, puts other on bottom. Knight may accept (keeps facedown) or decline (+5 Grit). Includes equipment like Elven Sword, Enchanted Bow, Javelin, Heroic Boots, Mighty Axe, Pixie Lantern, and Potion Kit.

Cards played on the Knight when she reveals an Event tile or enters a tile with an Event token. Cave draws 3, chooses 1 to play, puts others on bottom. Include harmful effects and the Cave Bread card that recovers discarded Hero cubes. 15 total; once exhausted, Events are ignored.
Knight Treasure card. Increases Perception by 1 and Strength by 1 during your turn. A strong combat and exploration upgrade.

Knight Treasure card. Shoot the Dragon or Thief from up to 5 tiles away in a straight line, even if the Dragon is underground or Dark tiles are in between. When shot, Thief is killed; Dragon discards Power cards equal to Knight's Strength minus 1.

Knight Treasure card. Next attack can target the Dragon while underground, and grants +1 Strength on that attack. Cannot be combined with Bomb on the same turn. One-time use (removed from game after use). Does not require a Hero cube.
Knight Treasure card. Increases Movement by 4. Massive mobility boost for exploration.

Knight Treasure card. Increases Strength by 1 during your turn. If you successfully attack the Dragon with a Hero cube on the Mighty Axe, discard the cube to deal 2 Health damage instead of 1. Cannot be used with Bomb underground.
Knight Treasure card. Increases Movement and Perception by 1 each. Balanced exploration upgrade.
Knight Treasure card. Remove from game and discard the Hero cube on it to regain 2 Health. Emergency healing option.
The four spaces touching a center space in the four cardinal directions. Diagonal spaces are never adjacent. All movement and distance counting is orthogonal.
All eight spaces around a center space — four adjacent plus four diagonal. Used by the Dragon die and powers like Scorch.

An object is visible to another when a straight line can be drawn between them in a cardinal direction without passing through walls, Dark tiles, or open spaces. Objects on Dark tiles can still be visible to each other.
Any space or terrain that blocks movement, except when allowed by special exceptions (Knight's Ancient Map, Thief's Climb). Canyon outer spaces and certain Terrain features are impassable.

When players are moved by other players' effects or tile removal. Knight: cannot move through walls, onto Dark tiles, or onto spaces with other players. Goblins: cannot move through walls or onto other players. Dragon: underground cannot go onto other players; surface same as Knight. Thief: cannot move through walls or onto other players.
Any edge without a wall on a Lit tile. When a tile is revealed, Dark tiles are placed adjacent to all open edges (unless during the Collapse).
All components are public except: Sidequest cards (Knight), Secrets cards (Goblins), Power cards (Dragon), Cave tiles in hand (Cave), and facedown/unrevealed pieces.

Optional variant tiles: Canyon, Lake, Magma, Mushroom Forest, Pits (3), and River. Placed the first time each round an Event tile is revealed. Each has unique effects on movement and combat. Not Lit or Dark tiles.
Terrain tile with a bridge space in the center and two impassable outer spaces. Bridge is treated like a Lit tile. Dragon (surface) and Thief (Climb) can cross impassable spaces in a straight line.
Terrain tile. Goblins lose 1 Population when entering. Once on the Lake, Goblins count Lake spaces as Lit tiles for movement. Other roles unaffected.

Terrain tile. Knight loses 1 Health if ending turn on it (ignored at 1 Health). Goblins lose 1 Pop per exit and go to 0 Pop if ending turn on it. Thief is killed if ending turn on it. Dragon is unharmed.

Terrain tile. Any player piece inside is protected from all outside effects and can only attack/be attacked by pieces in the same space. Blocks Knight's Bow, Goblins' Hex, Dragon's Hiss and Slap, Cave's Giant Bats, and Thief's Hand Crossbow.

Terrain tiles (3 pieces). When placed, one goes near the triggering player, others go far away. Each Pit is adjacent to the other two Pits but they don't create visibility. Players can never end a turn on a Pit.

Terrain tile with directional current arrows. Entering any River space allows free movement in the current direction. Moving against the current uses normal movement for Knight, Thief, and Dragon. Goblins lose 1 Pop per space against current.

Variant card used when there is no Cave player. At end of each player's turn, draw and place tiles equal to the greatest of: revealed Crystal tiles, Knight's available Hero cubes, largest Goblin Tribe Pop, Dragon's Spirit, or Thief's Movement. During Collapse, remove that many tiles instead.
Variant card. The Dragon starts at 6 Health (instead of 5). If the Dragon is not in the game, the Knight must smash 6 Crystals instead of 5.

Variant card given to the Knight in games without the Goblin player. Provides automated Goblin Tribe behavior using Monster tokens. Three difficulty lines: Normal Goblins (A), Tough Goblins (B), and Monsters (C).
Variant card given to certain players in games without the Dragon. Allows revealing Dark tiles without the Dragon's Flame power.
Variant card given to the Dragon in certain player combinations. Allows the Dragon to place Treasure tokens on the map, partially replacing the Cave's function.
